title: vicia tenuifolia subsp. dalmatica
intro: Vicia tenuifolia subsp. dalmatica, also known as bird's foot trefoil or Dalmatian vetch, is a perennial legume native to the Mediterranean region. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it beneficial for treating wounds, skin conditions, and digestive issues. The plant's leaves and flowers have been used to create herbal preparations, such as infusions and decoctions, which are said to provide health benefits including reduced inflammation, improved digestion, and enhanced immune function. In addition, the plant's leaves and flowers have been used to create teas, tinctures, and salves that are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
